ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT JACOBS CONSTRUCTORS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND ITS ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO ISSUES 1 AND 4 (NEGLIGENCE AND PREMISES OWNER/CONTRACTOR LIABILITY) AND OFF CALENDAR AS TO ISSUES 2, 3 AND 5 (FALSE REPRESENTATION, STRICT LIABILITY AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES). MR. FARLEY'S DECLARATION AND MR. MOSER'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING DURABLA GASKETS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. MR. MOSER'S DEPOSITION IS ADMISSIBLE PURSUANT TO THE DECLARATION AGAINST INTEREST EXCEPTION TO THE HEARSAY RULE. Off Calendar Mar-02-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
D. Paul Bird II, Esq., of McKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE (415-267-4175), appearing for defendant / moving party Jacobs Constructors, Inc.
Jennifer Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party James Carmichael

RULING:
DEFENDANT JACOBS CONSTRUCTORS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Jacobs Constructors, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 and 4 (negligence and premises owner/contractor liability) and off calendar as to issues 2, 3 and 5 (false representation, strict liability and punitive damages). Mr. Farley's declaration and Mr. Moser's deposition testimony create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing Durabla gaskets attributable to defendant. Mr. Moser's deposition is admissible pursuant to the declaration against interest exception to the hearsay rule. Plaintiff dismissed his claims for false representation, strict liability and punitive damages. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

RULING:
DEFENDANT LOOMIS EQUIPMENT COMPANY.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Loomis Equipment Company's motion for summary judgment is continued pursuant to C.C.P. 437c(h) to March 3, 2011 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 220. It appears from plaintiff's papers that facts essential to justify opposition may exist but cannot now be presented, namely a deposition of defendant's person most knowledgeable. Defendant Calpacific Equipment Company¿S Motion For Summary Judgment Is Continued On The Court¿S Own Motion To February 8, 2011 At 9:30 A.M. In Department 220.
Off Calendar Feb-08-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jose Anthony Montalvo, Esq., of CESARI, WERNER & MORIARTY, PC (415-391-1113, X-11), appearing for defendant/ moving party CalPacific Equipment Company
Jennifer Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party James Carmichael

RULING:
DEFENDANT CALPACIFIC EQUIPMENT COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant CalPacific Equipment Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing materials attributable to defendant. By failing to submit responses to contention discovery with its moving papers, defendant cannot successfully invoke the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Citing to and attaching plaintiff's responses to General Order No. 129 standard interrogatories is insufficient. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

RULING:
DEFENDANT MONTEREY MECHANICAL COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Monterey Mechanical Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing materials disturbed by defendant. By failing to cite to plaintiff's responses to defendant's special discovery in its separate statement, defendant cannot successfully invoke the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-03-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party James Carmichael

RULING:
DEFENDANT MCJUNKIN CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant McJunkin Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production because its separate statement does not cite to facts showing that it propounded all facts, witnesses and documents interrogatories on plaintiff, and that plaintiff's responses thereto were factually devoid. Even if defendant had done so, there is a triable issue whether plaintiff used, installed and/or removed gaskets supplied by defendant. Plaintiff's objection to the second sentence in paragraph 6 of Mr. Colonna's declaration is sustained. Order signed in open court.Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
